title: Aragallus lagopus
taxonomy: Aragallus lagopus is classified as a plant species within the family Fabaceae. This family is commonly known as the legume family. Aragallus lagopus is further classified as a genus within the tribe Loteae. The plant is native to North America and is part of the Fabaceae family, which comprises a wide range of flowering plants. The classification of Aragallus lagopus reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.

intro: Aragallus lagopus, also known as Wolf's Bane or Dragon's Breath, is a perennial plant species belonging to the legume family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting immune function. The bioactive constituents of Aragallus lagopus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, such as lupanine and sparteine, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in some folk remedies for its putative ability to repel wolves and other predators.
